definition: existing in large quantities; plentiful
example: The region has abundant natural resources.

definition: to obtain or get something
example: She acquired fluency in French after living in Paris.

definition: enough or satisfactory for a purpose
example: The hotel provided adequate facilities for a short stay.

definition: to publicly support or recommend
example: Many scientists advocate for renewable energy.

definition: to distribute resources for a particular purpose
example: The budget allocates funds for public schools.

definition: having more than one possible meaning
example: Her answer was ambiguous and confused everyone.

definition: to examine something in detail
example: Researchers analyzed the data for three months.

definition: to expect or predict something
example: We anticipate strong demand for the new product.

definition: clearly visible or understood
example: It was apparent that she had been crying.

definition: a way of dealing with a problem
example: We need a new approach to teaching math.

definition: based on chance rather than reason
example: The decision seemed arbitrary and unfair.

definition: to evaluate or judge the quality of
example: Teachers assess students' progress regularly.

definition: to accept as true without proof
example: Don't assume everyone agrees with you.

definition: to succeed in achieving something
example: He attained the rank of captain by age 30.

definition: an advantage or profit gained from something
example: Regular exercise has many health benefits.

definition: prejudice in favor of or against something
example: The study showed clear gender bias.

definition: truthful and straightforward; frank
example: She gave a candid interview about her failures.

definition: logical and consistent
example: He struggled to give a coherent explanation.

definition: complete and including everything necessary
example: The book offers a comprehensive review of the subject.

definition: an abstract idea or general notion
example: The concept of freedom varies across cultures.

definition: to organize and carry out
example: The team conducted experiments for a year.

definition: a result or effect of an action
example: She faced the consequences of her decision.

definition: notably large in size or amount
example: The project requires considerable effort.

definition: acting or done in the same way over time
example: Her performance has been consistent all season.

definition: to be a part of a whole
example: Women constitute half of the workforce.

definition: to deny the truth of a statement
example: His actions contradict his words.

definition: to give in order to help achieve something
example: Everyone contributed to the success of the event.

definition: prolonged public disagreement
example: The new law caused much controversy.

definition: to communicate or make known
example: Words cannot convey how grateful I am.

definition: extremely important
example: This meeting is crucial for our future.

definition: a formal discussion on a topic
example: There was a heated debate in parliament.

definition: to become smaller or less; to politely refuse
example: Sales declined in the last quarter.

definition: to reach a conclusion by reasoning
example: From the evidence we can deduce her intent.

definition: to state the exact meaning of
example: Please define your terms before arguing.

definition: to show clearly by example
example: The results demonstrate the effectiveness of the method.

definition: to show or represent in a picture or story
example: The film depicts life in medieval Europe.

definition: to obtain something from a source
example: Many English words derive from Latin.

definition: to make or become less
example: His influence has diminished over time.

definition: clearly different or separate
example: There are three distinct species in this area.

definition: showing a variety; very different
example: The city has a diverse population.

definition: having power or influence over others
example: English is the dominant language of science.

definition: to add more detail to something
example: Could you elaborate on that point?

definition: to become apparent; to come into view
example: New evidence has emerged in the case.

definition: to give special importance to
example: The teacher emphasized the need for practice.

definition: to unexpectedly meet or face
example: We encountered several problems along the way.

definition: to improve the quality or value
example: The new software enhances productivity.

definition: to make certain that something happens
example: Please ensure the door is locked.

definition: equal in value, function, or meaning
example: One dollar is equivalent to about 18 pesos.

definition: to set up or create permanently
example: The company was established in 1990.

definition: plainly seen or understood
example: His disappointment was evident.

definition: to develop gradually
example: Technology continues to evolve rapidly.

definition: to go beyond a limit
example: Sales exceeded all expectations.

definition: restricted; not shared
example: This club has exclusive membership.

definition: stated clearly and in detail
example: She gave explicit instructions.

definition: to make an action or process easier
example: Technology facilitates remote work.

definition: to rise and fall irregularly
example: Prices fluctuate with demand.

definition: forming a necessary base; essential
example: Honesty is a fundamental value.

definition: to produce or create
example: Solar panels generate electricity.

definition: truly what it is said to be; authentic
example: His concern seemed genuine.

definition: a proposed explanation to be tested
example: The scientist tested her hypothesis.

definition: to show the meaning more clearly by example
example: This example illustrates my point.

definition: to put a plan into effect
example: The school implemented new rules.

definition: suggested but not directly expressed
example: There was an implicit threat in her words.

definition: to suggest without stating directly
example: Are you implying that I'm lying?

definition: something that motivates action
example: Bonuses are an incentive to work harder.

definition: certain to happen; unavoidable
example: Change is inevitable.

definition: the capacity to have an effect
example: Parents have great influence on their children.

definition: existing as a natural part of something
example: There are inherent risks in any investment.

definition: to begin or start
example: The company initiated a new program.

definition: necessary to make a whole complete
example: Trust is integral to any relationship.

definition: to combine one thing with another
example: We need to integrate these systems.

definition: extreme in strength or feeling
example: The heat was intense in July.

definition: to explain the meaning of
example: Critics interpret the poem differently.

definition: to become involved to change a situation
example: The teacher intervened in the argument.

definition: to show to be reasonable
example: Nothing can justify such behavior.

definition: conforming to the law; valid
example: She had a legitimate reason to be upset.

definition: to cause to continue; to keep
example: It's important to maintain good relationships.

definition: an edge or border; the amount by which something is won
example: She won by a small margin.

definition: nothing more than
example: He was a mere child at the time.

definition: so small it can be ignored
example: The damage was negligible.

definition: a concept or belief
example: She had no notion of what to do next.

definition: to get, acquire, or secure
example: He obtained a scholarship to study abroad.

definition: to happen or take place
example: Accidents occur when drivers are careless.

definition: to extend over and cover partly
example: Our duties sometimes overlap.

definition: to bury or drown beneath a huge mass
example: I'm overwhelmed by all this work.

definition: similar or corresponding
example: There are parallel cases in other countries.

definition: to become aware of through the senses
example: She perceived a change in his tone.

definition: to continue firmly in a course of action
example: She persisted in her studies despite difficulties.

definition: a fact or situation observed to exist
example: Global warming is a worrying phenomenon.

definition: a course of action adopted by an organization
example: The company's hiring policy has changed.

definition: latent qualities that may be developed
example: She has great potential as an athlete.

definition: exact and accurate
example: We need precise measurements.

definition: present as the strongest element
example: The predominant color was blue.

definition: widespread in a particular area
example: This disease is prevalent in tropical regions.

definition: existing or coming before in time
example: No prior experience is required.

definition: to further the progress of something
example: We need to promote reading among children.

definition: to cause or give rise to
example: Her question prompted a long discussion.

definition: to follow in order to catch or attain
example: She pursued a career in medicine.

definition: based on reason or logic
example: There must be a rational explanation.

definition: to identify as previously known
example: I didn't recognize you with glasses.

definition: to strengthen or support
example: Praise can reinforce good behavior.

definition: closely connected to the matter at hand
example: Please provide relevant examples.

definition: unwilling and hesitant
example: He was reluctant to leave.

definition: worthy of attention; striking
example: She made remarkable progress.

definition: to look like or be similar to
example: He closely resembles his father.

definition: to find a solution to a problem
example: The dispute was resolved peacefully.

definition: to put a limit on something
example: Visitor hours are restricted in hospitals.

definition: to continue to have or keep
example: She retained her title as champion.

definition: to make known something secret
example: The test revealed a serious problem.

definition: extremely thorough and careful
example: The training was rigorous but effective.

definition: insufficient for the demand
example: Water is scarce in desert regions.

definition: sufficiently important
example: There was a significant drop in sales.

definition: developed to a high degree of complexity
example: The software uses sophisticated algorithms.

definition: clearly defined or identified
example: Please give specific examples.

definition: coming after something in time
example: Subsequent events proved him right.

definition: of considerable importance or size
example: He inherited a substantial fortune.

definition: enough for a particular purpose
example: We have sufficient food for the week.

definition: to put forward for consideration
example: I suggest we leave early.

definition: to give a brief statement of the main points
example: Can you summarize the article?

definition: to exceed or be greater than
example: He surpassed all expectations.

definition: to support, maintain, or keep going
example: Can the economy sustain this growth?

definition: to make determined efforts to deal with
example: We must tackle climate change.

definition: too long, slow, or boring
example: Copying the text was tedious work.

definition: an inclination or disposition
example: He has a tendency to exaggerate.

definition: a set of ideas used to explain something
example: Darwin's theory changed biology forever.

definition: complete in all aspects
example: She did a thorough job cleaning the house.

definition: to grow or develop well
example: Plants thrive in this climate.

definition: to make a thorough change in
example: The internet transformed communication.

definition: the process of change from one state to another
example: The transition to college can be difficult.

definition: to cause an event or situation to happen
example: The incident triggered a protest.

definition: being or happening at the end
example: Our ultimate goal is to end poverty.

definition: to experience or endure
example: She had to undergo surgery.

definition: to commit oneself to a task
example: He undertook the difficult project.

definition: remaining the same in all cases
example: The classrooms are uniform in size.

definition: being the only one of its kind
example: Every person has a unique fingerprint.

definition: to make practical use of
example: We should utilize our resources wisely.

definition: having a sound basis in logic or fact
example: That's a valid point.

definition: of very great extent or quantity
example: The Sahara is a vast desert.

definition: to make sure something is true
example: Please verify your email address.

definition: capable of working successfully
example: Is this a viable business plan?

definition: strong, healthy, and full of energy
example: He took a vigorous walk every morning.

definition: nearly; almost entirely
example: The task is virtually impossible.

definition: able to be seen
example: The stars are clearly visible tonight.

definition: found or distributed over a large area
example: There is widespread support for the plan.

definition: to produce or provide
example: The investment yielded high returns.
